title: End-of-Life / End-of-Support Software Detection

compliance.assurance_rationale: Identifying EoL/EoS software on the managed fleet is a key step toward replacing or upgrading components that are no longer supported, supporting unsupported-component controls.
compliance.controlObjective: Flags devices and versions that are end-of-life or out of support so the organization can plan upgrades before relying on unpatched software.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Report from UC-5.13.59 listing `eolStatus`, `eolDate`, and `deviceFamily` with ticket references for hardware/software refresh or upgrade projects.

dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim`. via custom scripted input polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/compliance/detail?complianceType=IMAGE` (deploy per docs/guides/catalyst-center.md § Custom Scripted Inputs). Default poll interval: 3600s (1h). Volume: ~1 event per device per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `imageCompliance` (string), `runningVersion` (string), `targetVersion` (string), `deviceFamily` (string), `deviceName` (string), `managementIpAddress` (string), `upgradeStatus` (string). CIM data model: Change.
app: `Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk` (Splunkbase 7538) + custom scripted input for SWIM / lifecycle API
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:swim" (eolStatus="END_OF_LIFE" OR eolStatus="END_OF_SUPPORT" OR eolStatus="END_OF_SW_MAINTENANCE") | stats count as affected_devices values(runningVersion) as versions by deviceFamily, eolStatus, eolDate | sort eolDate
description: Identifies devices running software versions that have reached or are approaching end-of-life or end-of-support status, using Catalyst Center lifecycle data.
value: EoL/EoS software no longer receives security patches. Identifying affected devices enables proactive upgrade planning before vulnerabilities emerge.
implementation:
Combine **SWIM inventory** (`GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-device-image-updates` and `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/image/importation`) with **lifecycle** fields exposed for your Catalyst Center version (Cisco has published lifecycle/EoL context in product APIs; consult the current Intent API reference for the exact EoL endpoint in your release — for example endpoints under software/lifecycle that return `eolStatus`, `eolDate` tied to `runningVersion` / platform). Your poller for `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:swim` should output normalized fields: `eolStatus` (for example `END_OF_LIFE`, `END_OF_SUPPORT`, `END_OF_SW_MAINTENANCE`), `eolDate`, `deviceFamily`, `runningVersion`, `deviceName`.

**Scripted input:**

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_catalyst_swim/bin/collect_swim.py]
interval = 86400
sourcetype = cisco:dnac:swim
index = catalyst
disabled = 0
```

Run daily (or weekly) to reduce API load; for critical fleets, add a more frequent EoL-only pass if the API supports a lightweight lifecycle query. Store credentials in Splunk’s credential store; use `POST /dna/system/api/v1/auth/token` for authentication.
visualization: Table (deviceFamily, eolStatus, eolDate, versions, affected_devices), timeline of upcoming EoL dates, single value of affected device count.
knownFalsePositives:
**Cisco extended support or partner support contracts covering devices marked as EoS.** Cisco may still offer extended support or partner support for a software version that the standard EOL/EOS database marks as end-of-support. Distinguish by checking the organization's active support contracts against the device's software version. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_eos_exceptions` lookup with devices covered by extended support agreements.

**Device running a later version than the EoS version but flagged due to hardware EoL.** The hardware platform may have reached End-of-Life while the software version is still supported. Distinguish by checking whether the EoL/EoS flag is for the hardware (platformId) or the software (runningVersion). Categorize hardware EoL and software EoS separately.

**Lab or staging device intentionally running old software.** Lab equipment may run legacy software for testing compatibility or reproducing customer issues. Distinguish by checking whether the device is in a lab or staging site. Suppress by filtering lab devices from the EoS operational dashboard using a `catalyst_excluded_devices` lookup.

**EoL/EoS database in Catalyst Center not current after delayed update.** Catalyst Center's EoL database may lag behind Cisco's official EoL announcements. A device may be flagged as EoL based on outdated data, or not flagged despite being recently announced. Distinguish by cross-referencing with Cisco's official EoL announcements at `https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/products/eos-eol-listing.html`. No SPL suppression — update the Catalyst Center EoL database.

references.title: Catalyst Center SWIM Compliance API — Cisco DevNet

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 7538) with the **device** modular input enabled to `index=catalyst`, `sourcetype=cisco:dnac:device`.
• Cisco End-of-Life/End-of-Support data available — either from the Catalyst Center compliance API (which includes EOX status) or from a manually maintained `catalyst_eol_dates` lookup.
• RBAC: service account with **NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE**.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
End-of-Life/End-of-Support detection uses device inventory data combined with EOX lifecycle information. The TA polls device inventory from `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/network-device`.

Key fields:
• `softwareVersion`: the running firmware version (e.g., "17.6.3").
• `platformId`: hardware PID (e.g., "WS-C3850-48P").
• `deviceName`: device hostname.
• `deviceFamily`: device family category.
• `softwareType`: software type (e.g., "IOS-XE").
• The EOX status may come from the Catalyst Center compliance API (`complianceType="EOX"`) or from a lookup mapping `platformId` or `softwareVersion` to EOL dates.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:device"
| stats latest(softwareVersion) as running_version latest(platformId) as platform by deviceName, deviceFamily
| lookup catalyst_eol_dates platformId OUTPUT hw_eol_date, hw_eos_date, sw_eol_date, sw_eos_date
| eval hw_eos_status=case(isnotnull(hw_eos_date) AND now()>strptime(hw_eos_date,"%Y-%m-%d"), "End of Support", isnotnull(hw_eol_date) AND now()>strptime(hw_eol_date,"%Y-%m-%d"), "End of Life Announced", 1==1, "Supported")
| eval sw_eos_status=case(isnotnull(sw_eos_date) AND now()>strptime(sw_eos_date,"%Y-%m-%d"), "End of Support", isnotnull(sw_eol_date) AND now()>strptime(sw_eol_date,"%Y-%m-%d"), "End of Life Announced", 1==1, "Supported")
| where hw_eos_status!="Supported" OR sw_eos_status!="Supported"
| table deviceName, platform, running_version, hw_eos_status, hw_eos_date, sw_eos_status, sw_eos_date
| sort hw_eos_status, sw_eos_status
```

Understanding this SPL:
• **`lookup catalyst_eol_dates platformId`**: Resolves hardware and software EOL/EOS dates from a manually maintained lookup. Populate this lookup from Cisco's official EOL listings at `https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/products/eos-eol-listing.html`.
• **`hw_eos_status`**: Distinguishes between hardware that has reached End of Support (no more patches/replacements) vs End of Life Announced (still supported but lifecycle announced).
• **`sw_eos_status`**: Same logic for software versions.
• **`where ... !="Supported"`**: Shows only devices with EOL/EOS concerns.

Step 3 — Validate
• **Cross-reference:** Check 3-5 devices against Cisco's official EOL listings to verify the lookup dates are accurate.
• **Catalyst Center compliance check:** If using the compliance API, compare with Catalyst Center > Assurance > Compliance > EOX results.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** EOL/EOS summary tiles (how many devices past EOS, how many with EOL announced), table of affected devices grouped by platform, and a timeline showing upcoming EOL dates.
• **Alert:** Trigger monthly for devices approaching EOS within 90 days. Trigger immediately for devices that have passed their EOS date without a replacement plan.
• **Budget planning:** Use the EOS device list to drive hardware refresh and firmware upgrade budget requests.

Step 5 — Troubleshoot
• **Lookup out of date:** Cisco updates EOL announcements regularly. Schedule quarterly reviews of the `catalyst_eol_dates` lookup against the official Cisco EOL listings.
• **Extended support not reflected:** Devices with extended support contracts may still be supported past the standard EOS date. Add an `extended_support_end` field to the lookup for these cases.
• **Lab devices flagged:** Lab equipment running old firmware is expected. Filter using a `catalyst_excluded_devices` lookup.
• **No SWIM events arriving:** SWIM requires a custom scripted input; verify the script is deployed and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages.
• **NULL or empty values in target version:** devices without a golden image assignment have no target; missing values indicate no SWIM policy is configured for that device family.
• **Fewer devices than expected:** check the service account role; credential access denied errors indicate insufficient permissions.
grandmaExplanation: We find devices running old software that Cisco no longer supports or patches, which means they could have unfixed security holes.
